title: Event-source acceptance closure and the browser connection-event seam — four source walls, the socket v0.2 vocabulary fill, and the CI wall extension
purpose:
The event-source integration pillar governed by the WPORT-4 section of `docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:92-103` at `2cfd6cf`. The arc's INTENT, quoted: route all six frozen wake sources through the WPORT-2 arbiter (`send_message`, `cast`, async-NIF/Promise completion, receive-timer fire, native completions, trapped exits); fill Apollo Biscuit's socket v0.2 browser event-delivery seam using "one event vocabulary with the native `connection_events.rs` hub, with snapshot-at-subscribe as the precedent for initial state"; acceptance shape — "Starting from a truly idle VM, each of the six sources independently causes execution without any external pump call. For each source, a single event queues one turn; a burst before that turn remains coalesced; the durable message/completion/exit is not lost. Subscription tests receive an initial snapshot and then events expressed in the shared vocabulary. Tests fail if any source changes Rust state but leaves the process ready and unexecuted." (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:101` at `2cfd6cf`).

SUPERSESSION NOTE, binding: the arc's WPORT-4 boundary evidence is pinned at `d9de35e` — five lands stale — and now asserts falsehoods about current bytes (it claims the exported `send_message` and `cast` methods "do not request a scheduler callback" and that Promise completion "calls `complete_async` without requesting a host turn"). This brief quotes the arc's intent and NEVER its line-pinned gap claims; every current-state citation below was re-read at the bytes at `2cfd6cf`. The reframe also adds `WPORT-3` to `depends_on`, superseding the arc's frozen WPORT-2-only dependency line ("Dependencies: WPORT-2.", `docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:95`): R1's acceptance walls ride WPORT-3's landed unified-deadline test seams and timer-fire closure (`a17be58`), so the edge is truthful and already satisfied (RATIFIED at tear, Ruling 5, 2026-07-17).

At `2cfd6cf`, all six frozen wake sources are ALREADY ARBITERED — closed by WPORT-2 (`c9bb64a`) and WPORT-3 (`a17be58`): `send_message` reconciles deadlines then consumes the scheduler's one-shot external runnable edge and requests one coalesced arbiter turn (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:131-141`; `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:395-405`, `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:422-432`, `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:667-671`; coalescing at `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:460-465`); `cast` spawns a transient cooperative sender and routes the same edge (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:284-292`; `crates/beamr/src/native/actor_cooperative.rs:140-165`; `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:198-209`); Promise settlement calls `complete_async`, consumes the edge, and conditionally requests the arbiter in one borrow-scoped sequence (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1082-1117`; `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:251-260`); timer fires ride WPORT-3's unified deadline callback, which delivers both due classes and makes exactly one arbiter request per admitted fire (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:930-980`); native completion envelopes divert through `deliver_native_async_completion`, which builds `{ok, V}`/`{error, R}` on the target heap and wakes it — including the heap-copy-failure fallback wake (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:223-251`, fallback at `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:246-248`); trapped linked-process exits are generated only inside an active drain (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:340-348`, `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:482-513`), where the in-drain latch deliberately suppresses a duplicate external edge (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:508-513`, `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:667-671`) and the fairness continuation carries any remainder (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:573-582`). Trapped exits cannot arise outside an arbiter turn at `2cfd6cf`; "consume in the current turn or its single fairness continuation" IS their correct arbitration, not a gap.

What is NOT closed: of the 18 CI-pinned wasm tests (`.github/workflows/cooperative-wasm.yml:41-60`), only host send and the timer classes carry source-specific idle-wake/coalescing walls (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1951-1989`, `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:2148-2282`, `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:2285-2621`). No test anywhere invokes `WasmVm::cast` or `register_async_nif`; no wrapper test settles a Promise for a parked native handler from true idle; the only trapped-exit pin drives the public production `run_native_until_idle` pump (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:258`; zero production callers workspace-wide — only test code drives it, via the test-only `drain_until_exit` loop at `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native_tests.rs:73-82`; the pin itself is `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native_tests.rs:596-672`). And no browser event-delivery seam exists: the socket v0.2 vocabulary fill named for WPORT-4 by the arc (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:103`) and by Apollo's R11 (haematite `main` `02b8592`, `docs/design/HAEMATITE-WASM.md:672-677`) is entirely absent from the wasm surface. WPORT-4 therefore pays the acceptance debt (R1), builds the vocabulary seam (R2), and makes both permanent in the Node gate (R3).

Binding law, verbatim: "NO-POLLING (Tom's ruling): event-loop-driven scheduling — run-to-idle, wake on message; a polling scheduler is a TEAR CONDITION (F-0d acceptance). Any timer whose job is 'check whether something changed' is a design error." (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:19-23`). It binds R2 with Apollo's own teeth: browser subscribers MUST be TOLD, never poll; no timer fallback ever.
task:
HEADLINE REFRAME — TEAR-BLESSED (Waffles' half GRANTED at the 2026-07-17 tear, torn at e84f188; Tom's half is the scope supersession of frozen arc text, joint authority, routed in Waffles' packet — HIS WORD IS THE DISPATCH GATE): WPORT-4 is an ACCEPTANCE-CLOSURE plus VOCABULARY-SEAM brief. All six wake sources are already arbitered at `2cfd6cf`; two independent ground efforts (Norn Sol xhigh session `1d779d48-45dc-4721-8b2d-20ba6e48f36e`; ultracode `wf_21fd39ef-e7b`, 6 tracers + 6 refuters + 2 mappers + synthesis, zero refuted verdicts) found zero routing gaps, and every citation in this brief was re-verified at the bytes at `2cfd6cf`. ZERO wake-path code changes are expected, and this brief PROHIBITS new wake-path scheduling calls: no new `request_external_turn`/`schedule_external_edge` call site may be added at any source mutation seam. Reopening routing risks redundant host callbacks and breaks the in-turn trapped-exit semantics; any apparent need to add one is a deviation — STOP and report, because that reframe reversal needs its own tear blessing. R2's new seam consumes the EXISTING arbiter surface only.

GROUND REFINEMENTS carried on the record, not papered over: (a) the send_message error-ordering seam — `sync_and_reconcile()?` at `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:138` runs between wake and `schedule_external_edge` (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:139`) and can strand the latched edge ONLY when host `clearTimeout`/`setTimeout` themselves throw, the same broken-host world where the arbiter fails loudly; RULED (Ruling 6): RATIFIED as the declared host-failure abort path — do NOT reorder; reordering would be a blind change to a load-bearing landed WPORT-2/3 sequence on zero evidence of a transient-throw world. Two riders bind R1: a code comment lands AT the seam naming the stranded-edge consequence and its host-failure precondition, and any future evidence of a transiently-throwing host reopens this as its own finding. (b) The trapped-exit LATENT gap: bytecode-process exits perform no link propagation (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:548-556`, `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:589-598`) — unreachable today because cooperative bytecode `native_services` injects no link/spawn/supervision facility (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:720-731`); RULED (Ruling 7): RECORD-ONLY — a debug wall for an unreachable path asserts nothing real; a comment at the `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:548-556` seam names the latency, an arc-board line records it, and the wall itself belongs to the future bytecode-linking brief that makes the path reachable.

Binding laws restated where they bind: NO-POLLING verbatim (purpose block; a recurring callback that checks whether something changed is a tear condition — R2's browser subscribers are TOLD, and the zero-poll census intent of Apollo's R11 is acceptance ground); CI extension is exact-name list plus exact-count summary bump IN THE SAME COMMIT as the tests (`.github/workflows/cooperative-wasm.yml:41-80`); ZERO new `#[allow]`; no `unwrap`/`expect`/`panic` outside `#[cfg(test)]`; lib.rs's borrow discipline — every scheduler/deadline-service borrow scoped and dropped before any host call — binds every new seam exactly as the WPORT-3 pen note states it (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:728-730` carries it in the bytes); believed state is not citable state — every current-state claim is file:line at `2cfd6cf` (or successor pin at dispatch), re-read at the bytes, never a research artifact.

TEAR RULINGS (Waffles the Terrible, 2026-07-17, torn at e84f188 — all nine questions ruled; folded here as decided text per WPORT-2/WPORT-3 precedent; he spot-verified the send_message sequence at `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:131-141`, the raw-x(0)/rejection semantics at `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:753-767`, and the exact-18 CI summary at the bytes himself):
(1) JS ABI: mirror BOTH native method names — `subscribe_connection_events`, `subscribe_connection_events_with_snapshot`, `unsubscribe_connection_events -> bool`; one-method-with-option is a browser-only second shape, exactly what the spec forbids. `SubscriberId` is NUMERIC (a disposable handle imports lifetime/drop ambiguity across the wasm-bindgen boundary the native contract does not have). Snapshot delivery is SYNCHRONOUS — single-threaded wasm makes the native no-interleaving contract hold by construction under sync delivery; an async snapshot creates precisely the synthetic-vs-real interleaving window the native contract prohibits. Reentrant-register-without-catchup carries over verbatim.
(2) Host identity: the host feeds `{node, peer_creation}` on up-ingress and `{node, reason}` on down-ingress — NOTHING else. Generation is NEVER host-supplied: the hub mints it locally (per-peer monotonic from 1), mirroring native ConnectionGeneration's never-on-the-wire law, so INV-ALTERNATION and generation-density are enforced at the seam rather than trusted from the host. An explicit `connection_replaced(node, new_peer_creation, reason)` ingress expands atomically into `Down(g, reason)` + `Up(g+1)` — the spec's "map richer fate states explicitly" honored. A bare double-Up without intervening Down is a LOUD typed error, never silently coerced.
(3) ConnectionDownReason: explicit mapping onto the seven native variants, NO local non-exhaustive extension — a browser-only variant IS the second model. The module contract carries the full mapping table; a browser fate that genuinely cannot map is a STOP routed to the native vocabulary owner (the native enum is already #[non_exhaustive]; any new variant lands THERE, keeping one model).
(4) Native-completion wall: the PAIR — the end-to-end wall (`start_async` + real Promise settlement) plus the direct `complete_async`-injection unit wall as its bisection (when the e2e wall reddens, the unit wall separates Promise plumbing from envelope delivery for the cost of one test). R1 is therefore FIVE pins and the exact final CI inventory is 18 + 5 + 3 = 26.
(5) SCOPE: Waffles' half of the blessing GRANTED — the reframe from wiring to acceptance-closure + vocabulary-seam is what the ground shows; the prohibition clause stays HEADLINE (it makes the reframe enforceable rather than aspirational, greppable in the diff). `depends_on` WPORT-3 RATIFIED (truthful, already satisfied at a17be58). Tom's half routes in Waffles' packet; FOLD NOW, DISPATCH ON HIS WORD.
(6) send_message error-ordering: RATIFIED as the declared host-failure abort path — no reorder (see the ground-refinements paragraph above for the two binding riders).
(7) Trapped-exit latent gap: RECORD-ONLY (see the ground-refinements paragraph above).
(8) Arc-doc refresh: KEEP the historical d9de35e pins; a supersession note in the arc's WPORT-4 section points to this brief's re-verification at 2cfd6cf ("five lands stale; boundary evidence superseded"). Rewriting the pins would claim a re-verification the arc document never performed — believed state is not citable state cuts both ways. The note is the amendment and landed in the artifact at fold time.
(9) WPORT-3 deadline pillar: PARALLEL — the browser+Worker probe validates already-landed timer code; R1's timer citations ride the landed bytes, not the probe. The pillar stays an open arc-board line on the existing terms.
TEAR-ADDED REQUIREMENT (R2): the ten-invariant classification table (wasm-seam-asserted vs inherited-by-construction) must appear in the module contract AND in the handoff, so the evidence packet is self-contained.
x(0) CORRECTION: RATIFIED as byte-accurate (raw resolved term in x(0) with advance-past-instruction on fulfilment; x(0) + ExitReason::Error on rejection; {ok,V}/{error,R} envelope exclusively native-side) — the frozen outline's framing was wrong at the bytes and this brief corrects it with citations.

requirements.title: Five source-specific acceptance walls (pair ruling): cast, Promise completion, native completion envelope, trapped exit
requirements.spec:
Add FIVE in-crate wasm-bindgen acceptance walls on the Node leg (Ruling 4: the native-completion source gets a PAIR — end-to-end wall plus direct-injection bisection wall). Each wall copies the counted-gauge pattern of the existing cardinality walls (counter-delta assertions per frozen transition, `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:2148-2282`) and obeys the shared shape: start from TRUE IDLE; one event produces exactly one arbiter request (`requests + 1`, `queued_now == 1`); a pre-turn burst coalesces to one request/execution; the durable payload is observed on resume; NO manual `run_step`/pump drives progress (the burst/re-edge precedent is `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1951-1989`).

(1) CAST — proposed name `cast_from_true_idle_queues_one_turn_and_coalesces_a_burst` (the tear may rename; renames update R3's inventory in the same commit). No test in the workspace invokes `WasmVm::cast` (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:284-292`) or the cooperative `CoopSenderHandle::cast` (`crates/beamr/src/native/actor_cooperative.rs:152-165`): WPORT-2's acceptance promised a table-driven wall "including `call` and `cast`" (`docs/design/beamr/briefs/WPORT-2.json:45`) and it is unfulfilled for cast specifically — this wall pays INHERITED DEBT and the handoff names it as such. Prove: one cast from true idle queues exactly one turn; a cast burst coalesces; the cast envelope reaches the actor's cast handler (durable, not lost); and a cast to a DEAD pid still requests one turn and is silently dropped — the documented BEAM-send contract (`crates/beamr/src/native/actor_cooperative.rs:140-151`).

(2) PROMISE COMPLETION (bytecode) — proposed name `promise_completion_from_true_idle_queues_one_turn_with_durable_result`. `register_async_nif` (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:167-187`) is never called by any test; the two actor-call pins use the synchronous handler (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1529-1543`, `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1864-1896`), and `vm.call`'s Promise requests its edge at call time — a different seam. Register a Promise-returning async NIF, drive bytecode into the suspended await, reach true idle, then settle: one settlement = one arbiter request via `start_promise_completion`'s edge-consume-then-request sequence (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1082-1117`; `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:251-260`); a settlement burst coalesces; fulfilment delivers the RAW resolved term durable in x(0) on resume — the bytecode advances past the NIF with the value itself, no `{ok,V}` tuple exists on this path (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:753-759`) — asserted via the process's observable result. REJECTION TWIN in the same wall: a rejected Promise maps to the error completion (settlement-to-completion mapping, `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1087-1102`) and the bytecode process EXITS with `ExitReason::Error` carrying the rejection term in its captured exit result (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:761-767`, surfaced at `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:548-556`) — assert via `await_exit`/the captured exit result plus the same counter deltas, never by expecting resumed bytecode to observe an error value. The `{ok,V}`/`{error,R}` tuple envelope is built exclusively for NATIVE targets and belongs to R1 item (3) only (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:223-251`). RATIFIED AT TEAR (2026-07-17): the frozen outline's R1 item 2 carried the native envelope's `{ok,V}`/`{error,R}` framing for this bytecode wall — a byte-level imprecision; this brief's raw-x(0) framing is the byte-accurate one.

(3) NATIVE COMPLETION ENVELOPE — proposed name `native_completion_envelope_wakes_parked_handler_through_the_arbiter`. Core tests prove envelope delivery only with the test playing host and driving drains manually (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native_tests.rs:992-1131`); no wrapper test settles a real Promise for a parked NATIVE handler from true idle with arbiter counters asserted. RULED (Ruling 4) — the PAIR: the end-to-end wall AND a direct `complete_async`-injection unit wall (proposed name `native_completion_direct_injection_wakes_parked_handler`) ride together; when the e2e wall reddens, the unit wall bisects Promise plumbing from envelope delivery for the cost of one test. End-to-end shape: a native handler starts host async work via `NativeContext::start_async` (`crates/beamr/src/native/native_process.rs:383-422`) against a registered Promise-returning host op, parks with `NativeOutcome::Wait`, the VM settles to true idle, the Promise settles, and the wall asserts one arbiter request, burst coalescing, and the parked handler resuming to read `{ok, Value}` / `{error, Reason}` via `recv` (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:223-251`). Cover the heap-copy-failure fallback wake (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:246-248`) IF a deterministic injection seam exists without production changes; otherwise record it as covered by core bytes only.

(4) TRAPPED EXIT — proposed name `trapped_exit_wakes_linked_supervisor_without_external_pump`. The existing pin proves trapped-EXIT delivery and restart but drives the production `run_native_until_idle` (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:258`; zero production callers workspace-wide) through the test-only `drain_until_exit` loop (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native_tests.rs:73-82`, pin at `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native_tests.rs:596-672`) — KEEP that core test; the new wall is the ARBITER-LEVEL proof. Arbiter-driven: inside one requested turn a native slice Stops (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:340-348`), link propagation delivers `{'EXIT', source, reason}` to the trapping linked survivor and wakes it mid-drain (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:482-513`, trapping arm `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:506-511`; trap/link seams `crates/beamr/src/native/native_process.rs:199-220`, `crates/beamr/src/native/native_process.rs:303-312`); the wall asserts the survivor either runs in the SAME host turn or surfaces at the fairness wall as `runnable_remaining` with exactly one coalesced FairnessMacrotask continuation (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:573-582`) — never a duplicate external turn (the in-drain suppression contract, `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:508-513`, `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:667-671`; core negative pin `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native_tests.rs:1363-1366`), and never an external pump.

SEAM INVENTORY (all existing; this requirement adds test code only): test-only `cfg(all(test, target_arch = "wasm32")) impl WasmVm` — `arbiter_counters` (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1436-1441`), `unified_deadline_snapshot` (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1443-1462`), `fire_unified_deadline_at`/`_token_at` (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1476-1501`); helpers `host_macrotask`/`host_microtask` (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1566-1576`), `spawn_waiting_mailbox` via `spawn_native_root` (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1593-1602`; `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm_native.rs:198-209`), the synthetic `Module` builder (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1604-1639`); public surface `send_message`/`register_async_nif`/`spawn_actor`/`call`/`cast` (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:131-141`, `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:167-187`, `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:244-255`, `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:264-277`, `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:284-292`). New test fixtures (an async-NIF caller module, a cast-target actor, a trapping supervisor pair) live in the test module. Counters are COUNTED GAUGES, never derived; no wall may manufacture a pass by subtracting or ignoring unclassified callbacks.

TWO TEAR-ORDERED SEAM COMMENTS (comment-only production edits; neither is a wake-path scheduling call, the prohibition is untouched): (1) Ruling 6 rider — at the `sync_and_reconcile()?`/`schedule_external_edge()?` sequence in `WasmVm::send_message` (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:138-139`), a comment naming the stranded-edge consequence and its host-failure precondition (host `clearTimeout`/`setTimeout` throwing — the torn-down-realm world where the arbiter already fails loudly); the ordering is RATIFIED, do not reorder. (2) Ruling 7 — at the bytecode-exit seam (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:548-556`), a comment naming the latent gap: bytecode exits perform no link propagation, unreachable today because cooperative bytecode receives no link/spawn facility; the guarding wall belongs to the future bytecode-linking brief.
acceptance: CAST: from true idle, one `WasmVm::cast` yields arbiter `requests + 1`, `queued_now = 1`, one execution, and the envelope observed at the actor; a pre-turn cast burst coalesces to one request/execution; a dead-pid cast still queues exactly one turn and drops silently with no error and no lost-state assertion failure

PROMISE COMPLETION: with a registered Promise-returning async NIF and the VM at true idle, one settlement produces exactly one arbiter request and the resumed bytecode observes the durable raw resolved term in x(0) (no `{ok,V}` tuple on the bytecode path, `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:753-759`); a settlement burst coalesces; the rejection twin makes the process exit with `ExitReason::Error` carrying the rejection term in its captured exit result (`crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:761-767`, `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:548-556`), asserted with the same counter deltas

NATIVE COMPLETION ENVELOPE: a parked native handler that called `start_async` is woken through the arbiter by a real Promise settlement from true idle — one request, burst coalesced, `{ok, Value}` and `{error, Reason}` both observed via `recv` on resume; no manual drain call appears in the wall; the paired direct `complete_async`-injection unit wall proves the same wake/coalescing/durability assertions with the completion injected at the scheduler seam (Ruling 4)

TRAPPED EXIT: an arbiter-requested turn in which the linked child Stops delivers `{'EXIT', source, reason}` to the trapping survivor and the survivor completes its restart logic either in the same turn or through exactly one fairness continuation; arbiter counters prove no duplicate external turn and no external pump; the existing core pin `supervisor_restarts_crashed_supervised_child_via_factory` remains green and unweakened

Every wall starts from proven TRUE IDLE (idle-wall precedent `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:1899-1949`), asserts exact counter deltas for every frozen transition, and contains no `run_step` loop, no recurring callback, and no timing-precision assertion
The four walls fail if their source changes Rust state but leaves the process ready and unexecuted — the arc's silence-attacking acceptance intent, realized as counter/payload assertions
The two tear-ordered seam comments are present at `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:138-139` and `crates/beamr/src/scheduler/wasm.rs:548-556` (comment-only; the prohibition grep stays clean)

requirements.title: Browser event-delivery seam: host-fed connection-event hub mirroring the native vocabulary, with snapshot-at-subscribe
requirements.spec:
Fill Apollo Biscuit's socket v0.2 in `beamr-wasm`. The spec ground is haematite `main` `02b8592`, `docs/design/HAEMATITE-WASM.md` — R11 verbatim (`docs/design/HAEMATITE-WASM.md:672-677`): "**R11 — Deliver events through the Artemis edge-triggered socket.** Browser subscribers MUST be TOLD through `SOCKET-EVENT-v0.2`, never poll; reconnection uses `SOCKET-CONNECTION-FATE-v0.2` (§9). Acceptance — the zero-poll census finds no periodic peer trigger, and a fake clock advanced without commit/connection input produces zero sync work; one timer-created pull fails." The socket table (`docs/design/HAEMATITE-WASM.md:894-899`) requires `SOCKET-EVENT-v0.2` to "mirror native `ConnectionDownHook`, `NodeUp`/`NodeDown`, `SubscriberId`, and snapshot-at-subscribe" with "no timer fallback" if told-not-polled delivery is absent, and `SOCKET-CONNECTION-FATE-v0.2` to be "connection-up/down/replaced input that wakes head reconciliation" using "the same event vocabulary as native, not a browser-only second model", mapping "richer fate states explicitly".

STRUCTURAL FACT the design must honor: distribution is COMPILED OUT of the wasm closure (`beamr-wasm` selects only `cooperative,json` with `default-features = false`; no `net` — `crates/beamr-wasm/Cargo.toml:18-21`, held by the two locked closure checks at `.github/workflows/cooperative-wasm.yml:17-20`), so there is no `ConnectionManager` in the browser. The hub is HOST-FED: haematite's carrier (or any JS host) TELLS beamr-wasm about connection fate through a new exported ingress; beamr-wasm provides the subscription surface and the vocabulary mirror, NOT connection management. RULED (Ruling 2) — the host-identity contract: the host feeds `{node, peer_creation}` on up-ingress and `{node, reason}` on down-ingress, NOTHING else; generation is NEVER host-supplied — the hub mints it locally (per-peer monotonic from 1, mirroring native ConnectionGeneration's never-on-the-wire law), so INV-ALTERNATION and generation-density are enforced at the seam rather than trusted from the host. An explicit `connection_replaced(node, new_peer_creation, reason)` ingress expands atomically into `Down(g, reason)` + `Up(g+1)`; a bare double-Up without intervening Down is a LOUD typed error, never silently coerced.

VOCABULARY, mirrored exactly from the native hub at `2cfd6cf`: `ConnectionEvent { Up(NodeUp), Down(NodeDown) }`, `#[non_exhaustive]` (`crates/beamr/src/distribution/connection_events.rs:254-265`); `NodeUp { node, generation, peer_creation }` where `peer_creation` — not generation — answers restart-vs-blip (`crates/beamr/src/distribution/connection_events.rs:226-240`); `NodeDown { node, generation, reason }` with generation always equal to the most recent Up generation (`crates/beamr/src/distribution/connection_events.rs:242-252`); the seven `ConnectionDownReason` variants (`crates/beamr/src/distribution/connection_events.rs:100-126`); `ConnectionGeneration` a LOCAL per-peer monotonic session counter starting at 1, never on the wire, never cross-node comparable (`crates/beamr/src/distribution/connection_events.rs:205-224`); `SubscriberId` opaque (`crates/beamr/src/distribution/connection_events.rs:323-325`); the cross-crate constructors/accessors exist expressly for consumers (`crates/beamr/src/distribution/connection_events.rs:267-321`) and the types are re-exported at `crates/beamr/src/distribution/mod.rs:18-20`. THE NATIVE VOCABULARY HAS NO `Replaced` VARIANT: the spec's "replaced" fate maps onto the native sequence `Down(g)` then `Up(g+1)` (INV-ALTERNATION, `crates/beamr/src/distribution/connection_events.rs:9-15`), with `peer_creation` as the restart-vs-blip discriminator — do NOT invent a browser-only third event. RULED (Ruling 3): browser transport failures map EXPLICITLY onto the seven native variants — NO local non-exhaustive extension (a browser-only variant IS the second model). The module contract carries the full mapping table; a browser fate that genuinely cannot map is a STOP routed to the native vocabulary owner, where any new variant lands in the native #[non_exhaustive] enum, keeping one model.

SUBSCRIPTION SURFACE: mirror the native pair — plain `subscribe_connection_events(callback) -> SubscriberId` plus the blessed late-subscriber `subscribe_connection_events_with_snapshot`, and `unsubscribe_connection_events(id) -> bool` (`crates/beamr/src/distribution/connection.rs:820-870`) — RULED (Ruling 1): mirror BOTH native method names plus `unsubscribe_connection_events -> bool`; `SubscriberId` is NUMERIC; snapshot delivery is SYNCHRONOUS (single-threaded wasm makes the native no-interleaving contract hold by construction; an async snapshot creates exactly the prohibited synthetic-vs-real interleaving window); reentrant-register-without-catchup carries over verbatim. The non-negotiable core stands: synthetic subscriber-local `Up` rows for every live peer delivered before any subsequent real event, invisible to other subscribers, no session missed and none double-seen (`crates/beamr/src/distribution/connection_events.rs:455-531`; the single-threaded wasm analogue of the dispatch gate, and whether reentrant-registers-without-catchup carries over verbatim, is part of that ruling). The implementation must state, in the module contract, WHICH of the ten named native invariants (INV-ALTERNATION through INV-NO-REPLAY, `crates/beamr/src/distribution/connection_events.rs:7-89`) are testably asserted at the wasm seam and which are inherited by construction from the host-fed single-threaded design — silence on any of the ten is a deviation. TEAR-ADDED: the same ten-invariant classification table appears in the HANDOFF as well as the module contract, so the evidence packet is self-contained.

DELIVERY IS TOLD, NEVER POLLED: subscriber callbacks are invoked synchronously with host-fed ingress; if delivery must wake VM-resident consumers, it consumes the EXISTING arbiter surface (the task-level prohibition on new wake-path scheduling calls binds here). No timer fallback exists on any branch — R11 blocks rather than degrades.

SCOPE WALL, verbatim (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:97` at `2cfd6cf`): "The socket governs event delivery; it does not expand this arc into native membership or distribution work." The fill inherits socket v0.2's two readiness bounds — idle-VM resume is WPORT-2's arbiter and runtime construction is WPORT-1 — both landed. DOWNSTREAM CONSTRAINT, named but not built: the banked WPORT-10 browser-carrier amendment rides exactly this vocabulary ("open/down/reconnect ride the same hub semantics, snapshot-at-subscribe for join state", `docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:197`); naming the WPORT-10 vocabulary constraint suffices — no extension code, no carrier work (`SOCKET-CARRIER-v0.2` is externally filled, owner Apollo Biscuit, `docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:195`).

PLACEMENT (proposed; the tear may relocate): a new `crates/beamr-wasm/src/connection_events.rs` module holding the hub, vocabulary mirror, and ingress/subscription exports, keeping `lib.rs` within house LOC discipline; wasm-bindgen exports wire through `lib.rs`. Zero new `#[allow]`; no `unwrap`/`expect`/`panic` outside tests; every borrow drops before any host callback invocation.
acceptance: The JS host can feed connection fate (up, down, replaced-as-Down-then-Up) into the hub and every subscriber observes events whose fields round-trip the native vocabulary exactly: node identity, generation (local, monotonic from 1, Down(g) matching the last Up(g)), peer_creation on Up, and a down reason drawn from the ruled `ConnectionDownReason` mapping

A replacement fed through the `connection_replaced(node, new_peer_creation, reason)` ingress is observed atomically as `Down(g, reason)` then `Up(g+1)` with `peer_creation` distinguishing peer restart from link blip; a bare double-Up without intervening Down produces the loud typed error; no third event variant exists anywhere in the seam's surface or serialized shapes

Late subscription with snapshot delivers one synthetic `Up` per live peer to the new subscriber ONLY, before any subsequent real event; existing subscribers observe nothing synthetic; a subscription during churn misses no session and double-sees none

`unsubscribe_connection_events(id)` with the NUMERIC `SubscriberId` returns bool and stops delivery; a subscriber that unsubscribes mid-stream observes no later event; snapshot delivery is synchronous and preserves no-interleaving

The module contract names all ten native invariants and classifies each as wasm-seam-asserted or inherited-by-construction, with a test or a stated structural argument per invariant; the same classification table is reproduced in the build handoff

No branch of the seam arms a recurring callback, timer fallback, or poll; delivery to VM-resident consumers reuses the existing arbiter surface with no new `request_external_turn`/`schedule_external_edge` call sites (the R2 diff is greppably free of both)
No native membership, distribution, ConnectionManager, or carrier code enters the wasm closure; the two locked wasm `cargo check` closures remain green

requirements.title: Subscription/snapshot acceptance walls and the exact-name/exact-count CI extension
requirements.spec:
Pin R2's seam in the Node wasm gate and extend the CI wall mechanically. SUBSCRIPTION WALLS (proposed names; the tear may rename): `subscription_snapshot_delivers_synthetic_ups_then_live_events_in_shared_vocabulary` — initial snapshot then live events, ordering strict (synthetics strictly before subsequent real events), asserting the shared-vocabulary field round-trip (the arc's acceptance sentence realized: "Subscription tests receive an initial snapshot and then events expressed in the shared vocabulary", `docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:101`); `subscription_synthetics_are_subscriber_local_and_unsubscribe_stops_delivery` — locality of synthetic catch-up plus unsubscribe teardown; `subscription_during_churn_misses_no_session_and_double_sees_none` — the no-gap-no-double wall under host-fed churn. All three MIRROR the native precedent corpus at the wasm seam (`crates/beamr/src/distribution/connection_events_tests.rs:1122-1169`, `crates/beamr/src/distribution/connection_events_tests.rs:1171-1205`, `crates/beamr/src/distribution/connection_events_tests.rs:1207-1310`) rather than inventing new semantics; where the tear's ABI ruling changes delivery timing (sync vs async snapshot), the walls assert the ruled contract without weakening no-interleaving.

CI WALL EXTENSION, same-commit law: `.github/workflows/cooperative-wasm.yml` machine-asserts an exact-name bash array (`.github/workflows/cooperative-wasm.yml:41-60`), per-name `grep -Fqx "test ${test_name} ... ok"` (`.github/workflows/cooperative-wasm.yml:62-67`), the exact summary string `test result: ok. 18 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 filtered out` (`.github/workflows/cooperative-wasm.yml:68-73`), and separate runner/assertion exit gates (`.github/workflows/cooperative-wasm.yml:75-80`). Every new pin from R1 and R3 — and any tear-renamed pin — is appended to the array with its fully qualified name, AND the exact-count summary is bumped from 18 to the exact final inventory, IN THE SAME COMMIT as the tests. RULED (Ruling 4): R1 is FIVE pins, so the exact final inventory is 18 + 5 + 3 = 26 — the summary string bumps to `test result: ok. 26 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 filtered out` and the name array gains all eight new fully qualified names in the same commit. A missing name, stale name, wrong count, or `no tests to run!` must still fail mechanically.

FAIL-FIRST AND STABILITY, WPORT-2/WPORT-3 precedent: perform, record, and revert (a) a mutation that breaks one R1 wall's coalescing (e.g. unconditional queueing at the seam under test) and show the wall fails; (b) a mutation that breaks snapshot locality (deliver synthetics into the shared path) and show the locality wall fails; (c) one stale/missing expected name fed to the workflow assertion and show the job fails despite otherwise-passing output. Run every new pin ten consecutive times in isolation. The existing eighteen names remain green and byte-relevant: the three conversion tests stay untouched, and no existing wall is weakened to accommodate a new one.
acceptance: SNAPSHOT-THEN-LIVE: the wasm subscription wall proves synthetic catch-up rows arrive before any subsequent real event and every event round-trips the shared vocabulary fields
LOCALITY/UNSUBSCRIBE: synthetic catch-up is observed by the late subscriber only; unsubscribe stops delivery under the ruled disposal shape
CHURN: a subscriber registering during host-fed churn misses no session and double-sees none, generation-dense per peer

`.github/workflows/cooperative-wasm.yml` names every final wasm test exactly and asserts the exact final summary (26 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 filtered out — Ruling 4) in the same commit as the tests; zero discovery, missing names, stale names, and wrong counts all still fail

All existing eighteen pins remain green; ten consecutive isolated passes for every new pin; reverted fail-first evidence for broken coalescing, broken snapshot locality, and a stale CI name list is captured in the handoff

boundaries: PROHIBITION (headline; Waffles' half of the scope blessing GRANTED at the 2026-07-17 tear, Tom's half is the dispatch gate): no new wake-path scheduling calls. All six frozen sources are already arbitered at `2cfd6cf`; no new `request_external_turn`/`schedule_external_edge` call site may be added at any source mutation seam, and no source's routing is redesigned. An apparent need to reopen routing is a STOP-and-report deviation.

NO WPORT-2/WPORT-3 surface changes: the drain contract, arbiter state machine, coalescing invariant, and unified deadline service are stable surface (`crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:460-582`, `crates/beamr-wasm/src/lib.rs:719-980`). R1 adds test code only; R2 consumes existing surface.

NO native membership or distribution work — the arc's scope wall verbatim: "The socket governs event delivery; it does not expand this arc into native membership or distribution work" (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:97`). No ConnectionManager port, no `net`, no carrier: `SOCKET-CARRIER-v0.2` is externally filled (owner Apollo Biscuit, `docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:195`) and WPORT-10 stays banked (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:197`) — WPORT-4 names the vocabulary constraint and builds nothing for it.
NO threads-surface changes; the wasm closure remains `cooperative,json` and both locked `cargo check` closures stay green.
NO browser/Worker platform claims in CI acceptance; the Node runner is the executed ground. The WPORT-3 deadline pillar's OPEN browser+Worker probe is a separate validation matter (open question 9).
NO JS-stub substitution in tests (arc wall): real VM, real wasm execution, real Promise settlement.

Laws stated where they bind: NO-POLLING verbatim — "a polling scheduler is a TEAR CONDITION (F-0d acceptance). Any timer whose job is 'check whether something changed' is a design error" (`docs/design/beamr/WASM-PORT-ARC.md:19-23`) — and Apollo's R11 makes it the R2 seam's own acceptance (told-not-polled, no timer fallback, zero-poll census intent); ZERO new `#[allow]`; no `unwrap`/`expect`/`panic` outside tests; every scheduler/deadline/hub borrow drops before any host call; CI extension is exact-name plus exact-count in the same commit; believed state is not citable state — every claim is file:line at `2cfd6cf` (or the successor pin at dispatch), re-read at the bytes.
verification:
BUILD-LEG ORIENTATION carries Tom's standing rule VERBATIM: "NO builds in temporary directories, ever." Worktrees go UNDER the repo (`<repo>/.worktrees/<name>`, with `.worktrees/` in `.git/info/exclude` — already added for beamr), and every build sets `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=<repo>/target` so the repo has exactly one warm target pile — no worktree ever grows its own. Logs go to the artifacts dir (`~/Developer/ablative/artemis-artifacts/...`), never loose /tmp files.

Run the house bar on the final head: `cargo fmt --check`; fresh-lint per the amended gate law (`cargo clean -p <crate>` before `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` and `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`, with the Compiling/Checking line captured beside the exit code — touch-before-clippy is inert on cargo 1.94.1; cached greens are stamped "checksum-verified cache hit", never "fresh re-lint"); `cargo test --workspace --all-features --lib`; `cargo test --workspace --all-features --test '*'`; `npm test --prefix examples/edge-worker`. Run both locked wasm checks (`.github/workflows/cooperative-wasm.yml:17-20`): `cargo check --target wasm32-unknown-unknown -p beamr --no-default-features --features cooperative,json --locked` and `cargo check --manifest-path crates/beamr-wasm/Cargo.toml --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --locked`. Then the pinned Node leg: `CARGO_TARGET_WASM32_UNKNOWN_UNKNOWN_RUNNER=wasm-bindgen-test-runner cargo test --manifest-path crates/beamr-wasm/Cargo.toml --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --locked -- --nocapture`; require the UPDATED workflow assertion to find every exact existing and new name and the exact final `26 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 filtered out` summary (Ruling 4: 18 + 5 + 3), with missing names, stale names, wrong counts, and `no tests to run!` still failing. Run every new pin in isolation ten consecutive times. Perform, record, and REVERT the three R3 fail-first mutations. Grep-verify the prohibition: the final diff adds no `request_external_turn` or `schedule_external_edge` call site outside test code, and adds zero `#[allow]` and zero non-test `unwrap`/`expect`/`panic`. Stamp the handoff with final `git rev-parse HEAD`, `rustc --version --verbose`, `cargo --version`, `wasm-bindgen-test-runner --version`, `node --version`, wasm target, UTC timestamps, exact commands, counter snapshots per frozen transition for all five R1 walls, the ten-invariant classification table from R2, the exact final test inventory, fail-first outputs, and the inherited-debt note (cast acceptance owed since WPORT-2.json:45). Record the arc-board lines: the WPORT-3 deadline pillar remains OPEN pending its browser+Worker probe run, proceeding in PARALLEL (Ruling 9), and the arc-doc supersession note (Ruling 8: historical d9de35e pins kept, note added in the arc's WPORT-4 section) landed with this fold. No browser/Worker observation is required or accepted as CI proof by this block.
